Notes: Cold 2026-07-03 verification shows this is a real pdfe text rendering issue, not a pdfe-better-than-reference case. pdfe renders readable text but the font shape/spacing is visibly wrong for the non-embedded CenturyGothic,Bold Identity-H font with explicit CID widths.

Target.Reason: The PDF declares a non-embedded CenturyGothic,Bold Type0 Identity-H font with explicit CID widths. Ghostscript is the best practical readable reference for spacing; Poppler is garbled and MuPDF substitutes a serif face, so the final target is the PDF font dictionary plus readable-reference evidence.

1.QualityReason: pdfe preserves the glyph sequence but uses the wrong fallback metrics/shape, producing visibly incorrect bold text spacing. This is an active rendering issue.
1.Notes: Verified by cold no-cache scan and fresh contact sheet against pdfe, MuPDF, Poppler, and Ghostscript on 2026-07-03.
